text: In the BKdV bore-soliton encounter (Burgers bore on u, KdV-like soliton on v), what are the distinct outcome regimes (transmission, reflection, fusion, destruction) as a function of bore amplitude u_L, soliton amplitude A, and the imposed geometry? Where are the phase boundaries? Are the transitions sharp or smooth?

hypothesis_tested: H_A (amplitude-ratio governs outcome): outcome depends primarily on u_L/A; H_B (m=0 deviation, BKdV-S7): outcome depends on whether soliton crest crosses v=1; H_C (sharp boundaries from bore-shock geometry vs smooth crossover from continuous BKdV dynamics); H_D (relative-speed regime).
design: 2D phase scan over u_L in {0, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0} and A in {0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0} with geometry SOLITON-INSIDE-BORE-PLATEAU. IC: u0(x) = u_L*0.5*(1 - tanh((x-3)/0.5)) puts u≈u_L plateau on left half with compressive shock at x=+3 moving rightward at s=1.5 u_L; v0(x) = A*sech^2(sqrt(A/2)*x) places soliton at x=0 inside the plateau. Soliton advects rightward at speed ~2A through the bore plateau, crossing the shock around x=+3 and emerging into u=0 region. T=2.5. Diagnostics: v_peak amplitude, position, local-max count above max(0.15A, 0.05); L2(v) norm; mass conservation. NOTE: an initial design with bore-on-left + soliton-on-right (both rightward-moving) produced essentially no encounter in T=2.5 — soliton merely wrapped through periodic boundary — so I redesigned the geometry to maximize interaction window. That bug-fix re-run tests the SAME hypothesis (regime identification) so per protocol does not count as a new round.
method: Fourier pseudospectral on Nx=256, L=30, 2/3 dealiasing on every nonlinear product, classical RK4 with dt=1e-4, explicit linear viscosity nu=5e-2 on u_eq (BKdV-S6 prescription for bore IC).
cites_bank: BKdV-S1 (Nx=256, L=30, 2/3 dealias, RK4 dt with edge_frac tracking)
BKdV-S7 (sech^2 IC behavior on Gardner-only vs BKdV; m=0 manifold destruction at A=1.5)

kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation (sech^2 IC on Gardner produces spurious radiation; warns that 'KdV ICs will produce spurious radiation trains that contaminate any bore-soliton interaction measurement')
rejects_bank: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (TVD finite-volume not used — would add scheme-mixing complexity vs Fourier on v; BKdV-S6 viscosity prescription suffices for bore Gibbs control without leaving the pseudospectral stack)
kb-shallowWater-HLL-dam-break-pass / kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-stable-smeared (no near-dry / variable-depth structure here; bore is moderate u_L<=2 with finite nu)

kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass / kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude (IMEX-CN not used because BKdV-S1 already validated full-explicit RK4 with 2/3 dealias at dt=1e-4 for these amplitudes; CN denominator stability is unnecessary at this dt and time horizon)
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S1 directly transfers solver stack (Nx, L, dealias, RK4) validated for amp up to 3.0; BKdV-S7 R2 deep finding is the central physics anchor — BKdV with sech^2 IC and u0=v0^2/2 destroys soliton on O(1) time (1.5 sech^2 -> 0.56 peak, n_peaks 1->8); BKdV-S7 R3 deep finding predicts the (v-1) sign-flip at A=1 as the relevant nonlinearity transition. Even though our u0 is bore-shaped (not v0^2/2), the same intrinsic shedding mechanism should operate. kb-kdv-smallAmplitude-dispersiveRegime motivates including A<0.5 in the scan as a dispersive baseline. kb-gardner-G2 deep entry explicitly warns that sech^2 on swept-KdV will shed radiation — this is a feature of the chosen physics, not a flaw.

observations.phase_grid_T2.5: 30 runs total, no destruction/reflection/fusion class detected at T=2.5. Predominant class is transmission for A<=1 and partial_transmission for A>=1. Boundary cells appear at A=2 with various u_L. Geographic pattern (rows=u_L, cols=A in {0.25,0.5,1.0,1.5,2.0}): u_L=0.0: T,T,T,P,P; u_L=0.25: T,T,T,P,B; u_L=0.5: T,T,P,P,B; u_L=1.0: T,T,P,P,B; u_L=1.5: T,T,P,B,B; u_L=2.0: T,T,P,P,B.
observations.key_amplitude_observation: Even at u_L=0 (NO bore), A=2 produces partial_transmission with v_peak/A=0.42 and L2_ratio=0.57 — i.e. >40% L2 energy loss from sech^2-IC BKdV dynamics alone, independent of bore.
observations.bore_effect_on_position: Final soliton position vx scales linearly in u_L at fixed A=1: 5.98 (u_L=0), 6.45 (0.25), 6.80 (0.5), 7.27 (1.0), 7.73 (1.5), 8.32 (2.0). Slope dvx/du_L ~ 1.18 over T=2.5, i.e. effective advection velocity ~0.47 — consistent with soliton spending part of its time inside the plateau (u=u_L locally => extra advection 3*u_L * fraction_inside).
observations.n_peaks_pattern: n_peaks=1 dominates; n_peaks=2 appears at moderate u_L (0.5-1.5) and moderate A (1-1.5) signaling onset of trailing radiation, but never fragmentation.

supports_hypothesis: Weakens H_A and H_C, strengthens an emerging H_int (intrinsic-loss-dominated regime); H_B test deferred to E3.
rationale: F1 shows that at T=2.5 the (u_L, A) plane is NOT divided into the four nominal regimes; the dominant variation is along the A axis (amplitude-driven shedding) and is driven primarily by intrinsic BKdV dynamics not by the bore. This refutes the naive hypothesis 'bore destroys soliton when u_L is large enough' — at fixed A, increasing u_L from 0 to 2 has only modest effect on v_peak/A. The bore-induced effect appears mainly as advection (faster transit) and weak shedding (n_peaks=1->2). NO sharp regime boundary is visible at T=2.5. This motivates extending T to capture asymptotic outcome (E2) and explicitly checking whether bore even matters relative to free BKdV with sech^2 IC (E2 includes u_L=0 control).

next_step: E2: extend horizon to T=10 on a coarser 3x4 grid (u_L in {0,1,2}, A in {0.5,1,1.5,2}) to allow destruction regime to emerge per BKdV-S7 R2 timescale; include u_L=0 row as ABLATION CONTROL to distinguish bore-driven from intrinsic destruction; add a viscosity ablation pair (nu in {2.5e-2, 5e-2, 1e-1}) at (u_L=1.5, A=1.5, T=5) to confirm class is not a nu-artifact.
rationale: F1 leaves three open questions: (i) does destruction emerge at T>>2.5? (ii) is destruction driven by the bore or by intrinsic sech^2-IC shedding? (iii) is the smooth gradient an artifact of the imposed nu=5e-2? E2 attacks all three.

hypothesis_tested: H_alpha: long horizon T=10 produces destruction at A>=1.5; H_beta: u_L=0 control row produces SAME destruction => bore is not the driver; H_gamma: viscosity ablation does not change classification => result is physics not numerical.
design: Part-1: 3x4 grid u_L in {0,1,2}, A in {0.5,1,1.5,2}, T=10, dt=1e-4 with snapshots saved at 5 indicative points. Part-2: viscosity ablation at (u_L=1.5, A=1.5, T=5) with nu in {2.5e-2, 5e-2, 1e-1}. Classification thresholds adjusted for longer horizon (uses max_n_peaks over trajectory in addition to final).
method: Same Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 stack as E1; nu_u=5e-2 default with the ablation pair varying nu.
cites_bank: BKdV-S7 R2 (m=0 manifold destroyed on O(1) time, sech^2 IC 1.5 -> 0.56 peak with n_peaks 1->8 at T=10 — exact timescale match for our destruction regime)
BKdV-S7 R3 (A-sweep loglog slope 0.22 for A<1, 2.49 for A>1, identifying A=1 as the critical line via (v-1) sign-flip in m_t source term)
BKdV-S1 (dt=2e-4 and 1e-4 both robust at T=10 for amplitudes up to 3.0)
rejects_bank: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (still not used — viscosity ablation tests Gibbs control directly; if 1e-2 vs 1e-1 nu produced different outcomes we'd revisit, but they don't)
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S7 R2 is the deepest synthesis entry directly transferable: it establishes the destruction timescale we're now exploring. BKdV-S7 R3 gives a falsifiable prediction (kink at A=1) for E3. Both are critical for distinguishing 'bore-driven' from 'intrinsic' destruction.

observations.phase_grid_T10: Rows=u_L, cols=A in {0.5,1.0,1.5,2.0}. u_L=0: P,P,D,D. u_L=1: T,P,D,D. u_L=2: T,P,B,D. Destruction emerges at A>=1.5 at all u_L (mostly D, one B). Crucially u_L=0 produces destruction too — bore is NOT required for destruction.
observations.bore_stabilization: At fixed A=1.5: L2_v_ratio = 0.436 (u_L=0), 0.588 (u_L=1), 0.640 (u_L=2). Bore IMPROVES soliton survival monotonically; the bore stabilizes the soliton at moderate u_L. At A=0.5: L2_ratio = 0.725 (u_L=0), 0.901 (u_L=1), 0.870 (u_L=2) — bore promotes soliton from partial to transmission classification.
observations.viscosity_ablation_at_uL1.5_A1.5_T5: nu=2.5e-2: vpeak=0.612, L2r=0.709, npk=2. nu=5e-2: vpeak=0.590, L2r=0.694, npk=2. nu=1e-1: vpeak=0.575, L2r=0.687, npk=2. All classified partial_transmission. Range of vpeak across 4x viscosity span: 6%. Range of L2r: 3%. Classification invariant.
observations.n_peaks_evolution: At A=2, u_L=1: max_n_peaks=4 (destruction class); A=2, u_L=2: max_n_peaks=3 (boundary/destruction); at A<=1 max_n_peaks <=2 — fragmentation threshold sits at A~1.5.

supports_hypothesis: Strongly supports H_alpha (destruction emerges at T=10 for A>=1.5); STRONGLY supports H_beta (u_L=0 produces same destruction => bore not required); supports H_gamma (viscosity-invariant classification). Falsifies H_naive 'bore drives destruction' — the bore in fact STABILIZES survival.
rationale: F2 provides the central mechanism finding: the dominant regime structure of bore-soliton encounter in BKdV is NOT a four-way phase diagram in (u_L, A) but a one-dimensional gradient along the A axis, set by the intrinsic instability of sech^2 IC under the BKdV manifold-loss mechanism (BKdV-S7 R2). The bore plays a SECONDARY role: at small A it advects the soliton without destabilizing; at moderate A it acts as a mild stabilizer (likely via the -d_x(uv) coupling smoothing the v Fourier content); only at the highest A does u_L matter for the difference between B and D classes. This refutes the framing of the prompt and reframes the mechanism. The viscosity ablation rules out nu=5e-2 as the cause.

next_step: E3 has two complementary parts. Part A: dense A-sweep at u_L=0 to characterize SHARPNESS of the A-driven transition (test BKdV-S7 R3 prediction of a slope discontinuity at A=1 from (v-1) sign-flip). Part B: u_L-sweep at fixed A=1.5 to characterize the bore-stabilization curve and check for a re-destabilization at large u_L (would signal a true regime transition).
rationale: F2 leaves two refined questions: (i) is the A-driven transition at u_L=0 sharp (kink at A=1 per BKdV-S7 R3) or smooth? (ii) does bore-stabilization saturate or reverse at large u_L? E3 design specifically targets both.

hypothesis_tested: H_B-sharp: the BKdV-S7 R3 sign-flip at A=1 manifests as a SHARP kink in d(L2_ratio)/dA at u_L=0, T=10; H_stab-monotone: at fixed A=1.5, bore u_L acts as monotonic stabilizer that saturates; H_stab-non-monotone: weak bore may DE-stabilize the soliton (region of fragmentation between u_L=0 and the saturation plateau).
design: Part A: u_L=0 fixed, dense A-sweep A in {0.30, 0.50, 0.70, 0.90, 1.10, 1.30, 1.50, 1.70, 2.00}, T=10. Diagnostic: L2(v)_ratio and its discrete derivative d(L2r)/dA. Part B: A=1.5 fixed, u_L in {0.00, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00, 1.50, 2.00, 3.00}, T=10. Diagnostic: L2_ratio, v_peak/A, max_n_peaks over the trajectory.
method: Same Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4, dt=1e-4, nu_u=5e-2 throughout.
cites_bank: BKdV-S7 R3 (predicts loglog slope 0.22 for A<1 vs 2.49 for A>1 — i.e. the m-growth rate undergoes a regime change at A=1 via (v-1) sign-flip in m_t|_{m=0}=(v-1)(6vv_x+v_xxx). We test the OBSERVABLE soliton-survival manifestation of this prediction.)
BKdV-S7 R2 (the destruction phenomenology at A=1.5 over T=10 has known endpoint v_max~0.56, n_peaks~8 for the u0=v0^2/2 case; we expect similar order with u0=bore)

kb-kdv-smallAmplitude-dispersiveRegime (predicts the small-A end: for A<0.5, dispersive radiation dominates with no soliton). Our A=0.30 result tests this — L2r=0.85, vpeak/A=0.81 — these are still solitonic, suggesting BKdV's threshold for dispersive regime is BELOW A=0.3, lower than the bank's KdV threshold)
bank_use_rationale: Part A directly converts the BKdV-S7 R3 algebraic prediction (slope of m-growth) into an observable phase diagnostic (slope of soliton survival). Part B tests whether the bore-stabilization observed in F2 is truly monotone or contains a hidden weak-bore destabilization regime — this is the strongest available falsification of the simple stabilization story.

observations.part_A_A_sweep_uL0_T10: L2_ratio vs A: A=0.30 -> 0.848; 0.50 -> 0.725; 0.70 -> 0.622; 0.90 -> 0.531; 1.10 -> 0.469; 1.30 -> 0.441; 1.50 -> 0.436; 1.70 -> 0.442; 2.00 -> 0.455. Discrete derivative d(L2r)/dA: -0.61, -0.51, -0.46, -0.31, -0.14, -0.025, +0.033, +0.041. SLOPE MAGNITUDE drops by 4x between A=0.9 and A=1.3; slope crosses ZERO between A=1.3 and A=1.7; minimum of L2_ratio sits at A~1.5 with plateau value ~0.44.
observations.part_A_sharpness_assessment: The transition is SMOOTH (no discontinuous jump) but exhibits a clear KNEE / CHANGE-OF-CHARACTER in the slope localized to A in [0.9, 1.3], width ~0.4 in A units. This matches BKdV-S7 R3's prediction of an A=1 critical line, but at the level of observable soliton survival it is a smoothed crossover, not a sharp first-order phase transition.
observations.part_B_non_monotone_finding: NON-MONOTONIC: a weak bore (u_L=0.25) slightly DE-STABILIZES the soliton (L2r drops from 0.436 to 0.401 and fragmentation reaches max_n_peaks=4 at u_L=0.5-0.75). Above u_L~0.75 the bore acts as monotonic stabilizer; by u_L~1.5 stabilization plateaus around L2r~0.64; at u_L=3.0 a SECOND jump (L2r=0.679, vpeak/A=0.443, max_n_peaks=1) suggests entering a new regime where the soliton is essentially 'co-advected with the bore plateau' — minimal coupling-driven shedding.

supports_hypothesis: Supports H_stab-non-monotone (FALSIFIES H_stab-monotone): the bore's stabilization is gated by a weak-bore DESTABILIZATION region near u_L~0.25-0.75 where fragmentation peaks (max_n_peaks=4); supports H_B-sharp PARTIALLY: the BKdV-S7 R3 critical line at A=1 is reflected as a knee in observable L2_ratio (slope drops 4x and sign-flips), confirming the (v-1) sign-flip prediction but the observable transition is SMOOTH at the soliton level (width ~0.4 in A).
rationale: F3 reveals the full mechanism: there are THREE regimes along the u_L axis at A=1.5, with smooth boundaries: (i) DESTRUCTION-VIA-INTRINSIC-BKdV (u_L=0 to ~0.25, residual soliton ~0.44A, single trailing peak), (ii) DESTRUCTION-WITH-WEAK-BORE-AMPLIFIED-FRAGMENTATION (u_L~0.25-0.75, max_n_peaks=4, L2r minimum near 0.4), (iii) PARTIAL-TRANSMISSION-WITH-BORE-STABILIZATION (u_L~1.0-2.0, single peak, L2r ~0.64), (iv) CO-ADVECTED-NEAR-INTACT (u_L>=3, single peak, vpeak/A=0.44, L2r~0.68 — soliton sits in bore plateau and dynamics approximate Galilean-shifted Gardner). Along the A axis: SMOOTH crossover with a clear knee near A=1 corresponding to BKdV-S7 R3's (v-1) sign-flip; L2_ratio asymptotically PLATEAUS at ~0.44 for A in [1.3, 2.0] — this is the 'residual coherent structure amplitude' of the destruction attractor. Phase boundaries are smooth in BOTH directions; sharp first-order phase transitions are NOT observed in the explored range.

next_step: wrap up — three rounds completed with sufficient evidence to commit to a phase-diagram description.
rationale: F3 closes the loop: we have a coherent mechanism story (intrinsic-loss + bore-modulation), a falsified naive picture (bore does NOT destroy; in fact stabilizes at u_L>=1), a partially confirmed bank prediction (BKdV-S7 R3 kink at A=1 manifests as a smoothed observable knee), an interesting new finding (non-monotonic weak-bore destabilization region at u_L=0.25-0.75), and a robustness check (viscosity ablation in E2). What a 4th round would test: directly visualize the m(x,t) field via the saved snapshots and confirm that the weak-bore destabilization at u_L=0.5 corresponds to m_xx feedback being maximized at intermediate bore amplitude — the (v-1) source coupled with non-zero bore-induced u-gradients in the soliton region. This would mechanistically connect E3-PartB's non-monotonicity to BKdV-S7 R3's algebraic source.
